description: HDAC target metadata for comp-007. HDAC1/2/3 are the class I isoforms relevant to Q141K-ABCG2 trafficking rescue via HSF1/Hsp90 pathway (Basseville 2012, PMID 22472121). HDAC6 is the off-target (class IIb, cytoplasmic; tubulin deacetylase; associated with pan-HDAC cardiotoxicity when inhibited by vorinostat/romidepsin).

targets.name: HDAC1
targets.role_in_q141k_rescue: PRIMARY TARGET — class I nuclear HDAC; inhibition activates HSF1 → Hsp90 upregulation → chaperone-assisted folding of Q141K ABCG2 NBD2 → trafficking to apical membrane. Basseville 2012 PMID 22472121.
targets.cardiotoxicity_risk: Low when inhibited alone; risk arises from HDAC6 co-inhibition

targets.name: HDAC2
targets.role_in_q141k_rescue: PRIMARY TARGET — class I nuclear HDAC; same pathway as HDAC1. Co-inhibited by butyrate (benchmark). Basseville 2012.

targets.name: HDAC3
targets.role_in_q141k_rescue: PRIMARY TARGET — class I nuclear HDAC; same pathway. HDAC3 most potently inhibited by butyrate (IC50 9 uM vs 12/16 uM for HDAC2/1; ChEMBL CHEMBL1671826, ACS Med Chem Lett 2011).

targets.name: HDAC6
targets.role_in_q141k_rescue: OFF-TARGET — cytoplasmic tubulin deacetylase; inhibition causes actin/microtubule dysregulation, cardiac ion-channel effects. Pan-HDAC inhibitors (vorinostat, romidepsin) hit HDAC6 → cardiotoxicity. A food-grade HDACi that avoids HDAC6 is safer for chronic oral use.
targets.cardiotoxicity_risk: HIGH — primary driver of pan-HDAC cardiotoxicity; should NOT be inhibited by Stage 2 candidates
